lineup_source: Projected England XI, 2026 World Cup cycle (4-3-3): Pickford; James, Stones, Konsa, O'Reilly; Rice, Anderson, Bellingham; Madueke, Kane, Gordon

players.name: Jordan Pickford
players.note: Born in Washington, Tyne and Wear, to Lee and Sue Pickford; no documented foreign heritage.

players.name: Reece James
players.note: Born in Redbridge, London; he is of Grenadian and Dominican descent through father Nigel James, English through mother Emma.

players.name: John Stones
players.note: Born in Barnsley to Peter and Janet Stones, a working-class Yorkshire family with no documented foreign heritage.

players.name: Ezri Konsa
players.note: Born in Newham, London, to a DR Congolese father and an Angolan mother; two languages were spoken at home.

players.name: Nico O'Reilly
players.note: Born in Manchester, raised by mother Holli; his father is of Jamaican descent.

players.name: Declan Rice
players.note: Born in Kingston upon Thames; parents Sean and Stephanie were both raised in London, though paternal grandparents were from County Cork, Ireland (grandparent-level heritage only).

players.name: Elliot Anderson
players.note: Born in Whitley Bay to Iain and Helen Anderson; a paternal grandmother was born in Glasgow, but parents themselves are English.

players.name: Jude Bellingham
players.note: Born in Stourbridge; father Mark Bellingham is English (with a distant Irish-surname grandmother), mother Denise is of Kenyan descent.

players.name: Noni Madueke
players.note: Born in Barnet, London, to Nigerian (Igbo) parents; father Ifeanyi Madueke is his agent.

players.name: Harry Kane
players.note: Born in London; father Patrick Kane was born in County Galway, Ireland, and emigrated to London.

players.name: Anthony Gordon
players.note: Born in Kirkdale, Liverpool, to Keith and Nadine Gordon; family is described as of Irish and Scottish descent, but no documented evidence either parent was born abroad.
